title: Debug & Troubleshoot
steps: Reproduce the issue: get exact error message, stack trace, or failing test command
Search for prior knowledge: `notes_search(errorMessage or symptom)` — someone may have hit this before
Check related tasks: `tasks_search(symptom)` — the bug may already be tracked or have context
Locate the failing code: `code_search(functionName or errorKeyword)` then `code_get_symbol(id)` for full source
Understand the call chain: `code_get_file_symbols(filePath)` on the file, then `code_search` callers/dependencies
Check if docs describe expected behaviour: `docs_search(featureArea)` or `docs_cross_references(symbol)`
Check linked tasks on the affected area: `tasks_find_linked(filePath)` — recent changes may have introduced the bug
Identify root cause and implement the fix following project rules: strict TS, no `any`, port/adapter boundaries
Write a regression test in `tests/` that fails without the fix and passes with it
Run `npm test` — all tests must pass
Run `npm run build` if UI code was touched
Capture the root cause and fix as a knowledge note if non-obvious: `notes_create` + `notes_create_link` to affected code
If this is a recurring pattern, save a troubleshooting skill: `skills_create` with the diagnosis steps
inputHints: Error message, stack trace, or failing test output
Steps to reproduce or affected feature area
